title: sapota achras var. depressa
intro: Sapota achras var. depressa, also known as the West Indian sapodilla or black sapote, is a variety of the Sapota achras tree, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The sapote has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, and is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing cholesterol levels and improving immune function. Herbal preparations of Sapota achras var. depressa include extracts, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and ointments for topical application.
